title: 10 best herbal creams for breastfeeding breast pain

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because it contains calendula, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that soothes and calms irritated breasts. The cream's cooling and moisturizing properties reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by engorgement or clogged ducts, allowing for more comfortable feeding sessions and improved milk flow. Additionally, calendula's antimicrobial properties may help prevent infection and promote healing, making it a natural and effective remedy for breastfeeding-related breast pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the breast tissue, providing relief from painful engorgement.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in pot marigold creams protect the breast tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to breast pain and discomfort during breastfeeding.

Calming and soothing properties: Pot marigold creams have a calming and soothing effect on the skin, helping to reduce irritation, redness, and itching associated with breast pain, making it easier for new mothers to breastfeed comfortably.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and discomfort associated with breast pain during lactation.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that help to soothe and calm the breasts, providing relief from soreness and tenderness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers in early morning or evening when dew is still present on petals for optimal potency.
Dry calendula flowers in a single layer on paper bags or cloth screens for 7-10 days to prevent mold growth.
Combine dried calendula flowers with 200ml of sweet almond oil and 20ml of beeswax in a double boiler for infusing process.
Heat mixture over low heat for 2 hours, allowing infused calendula mixture to cool before straining through cheesecloth into sterilized containers.
Add 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil and 10 drops of lavender essential oil to the cooled cream base, mixing well for final preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Meadowsweet creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as salicin, work to reduce swelling and discomfort in the breasts, making it an effective remedy for engorgement and mastitis. Additionally, meadowsweet creams can help to relax the milk ducts, reducing pressure and tension that can cause pain during breastfeeding. This natural relief can help new mothers to feel more comfortable and confident while nourishing their babies.
remedy_properties: Methanolic extract: Meadowsweet contains methanolic extract, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in reducing breast pain caused by inflammation or soreness during lactation.

Salicylic acid: The salicylic acid present in meadowsweet cream has been shown to have a similar mechanism of action as aspirin, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding-related mastitis or nipple trauma.

Glycosides: Meadowsweet contains glycosides, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to soothe and calm breast tissue, reducing pain and discomfort caused by engorgement, swelling, or hormonal changes during lactation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of meadowsweet are used in creams to soothe and calm breast tissue, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
Flowers: MeadowSweet flowers contain salicylates, which provide anti-inflammatory properties to relieve nipple soreness and engorgement often experienced during breastfeeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried filipendula ulmaria flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Combine the ground herbs with 20ml of cold-pressed coconut oil and 10ml of beeswax in a double boiler set over simmering water for 10 minutes.
Add 5ml of vitamin e oil and stir until melted and well combined, then remove from heat and let cool to 60°c for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding any solids, and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.
Apply the cream topically to sore breasts as needed, gently massaging a small amount into the affected area with a circular motion for 2-3 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they provide natural relief from engorgement, swelling, and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger work to reduce inflammation and ease the pressure on milk ducts, allowing for more comfortable feeding sessions and reduced nipple soreness. Additionally, the warming sensation of the cream increases blood flow to the breasts, helping to stimulate milk letdown and aid in the removal of excess milk, further reducing breast pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the breasts, making it a natural remedy for engorgement and discomfort.

Analgesic and antispasmodic properties: The analgesic (pain-relieving) and antispasmodic properties of ginger help to alleviate breast pain and spasms associated with breastfeeding, allowing mothers to feed their babies more comfortably.

Antioxidant and tissue repair effects: Ginger's antioxidant properties help to protect the breast tissue from oxidative stress and promote its natural healing process, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with breastfeeding breast pain.
Roots: Containing powerful analgesic properties, roots provide relief from sharp pains and soreness experienced by breastfeeding mothers.
remedy_recipe: Grate 2-3 inches of fresh ginger root and combine with 1 cup of coconut oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil is infused with the ginger essence.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, pressing on the solids to extract as much oil as possible.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter to the strained oil and melt over low heat, stirring until smooth and combined.
Pour the mixture into sterilized tubes or containers and let cool and solidify completely, taking approximately 30-45 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the breasts. The herb's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, work to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort caused by engorgement, nipple soreness, and plugged ducts. By applying the cream directly to the affected area, new mothers can experience relief from breast pain and discomfort, allowing them to enjoy a more comfortable and successful breastfeeding experience.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to breast pain during breastfeeding.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as hyperforin and flavonoids, may help alleviate breast engorgement, swelling, and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.

Pain-relieving properties: St John's Wort's analgesic and anxiolytic (mood-calming) effects can help reduce pain and discomfort caused by nipple soreness, cracked nipples, or mastitis during breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in active compounds that help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with breastfeeding-related breast pain.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing breast pain.
Flowers: Known for their sedative effects, the flowers of St John's Wort are used to calm nipple soreness and discomfort caused by breastfeeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried st john's wort flowers according to good manufacturing practices guidelines.
Combine 20 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers with 100 grams of sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Infuse the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place shaking daily allowing solubilization and extraction of bioactive compounds.
Filter the infused liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding solids and ensuring clarity.
Mix the filtered liquid with 200 grams of beeswax pellets and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler melting and blending until smooth consistency is achieved.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The gentle application of these creams to sore nipples can provide instant relief, reducing discomfort and promoting a more comfortable feeding experience for both mother and baby. The calming effects of lavender also help to soothe the milk ducts, reducing swelling and tension that may contribute to breast pain during lactation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compound, linalool, helps reduce swelling and discomfort caused by engorgement or mastitis, allowing for more comfortable feeding sessions.
Soothing and calming property: Lavender's relaxing properties help calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate breast pain and discomfort, making it easier to breastfeed.
Antispasmodic property: Lavender's ability to relax muscles helps ease spasms and cramping in the breasts, allowing for more comfortable breastfeeding and reduced discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and magnesium, lavender roots help soothe and calm sore breast tissue.
Leaves: The leaves contain antioxidants that reduce inflammation and promote healing, alleviating breast pain associated with lactation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 tablespoon of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Combine the dried flowers and carrier oil in a clean glass jar, allowing it to steep for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Melt 1/2 cup of beeswax and mix with 1/2 cup of shea butter, then combine with the infused oil and stir until smooth and well combined.
Pour the mixture into sterile glass jars, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature before use within 6 months or storing in an airtight container for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because it cools and soothes the breasts, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The menthol in peppermint has a natural analgesic effect, numbing the pain associated with engorgement, swelling, and nipple soreness. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and heat, allowing mothers to breastfeed more comfortably and alleviate the stress and discomfort that can come with it.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Peppermint's menthol content reduces swelling and inflammation in the breasts, providing relief from engorgement and soreness.
Analgesic properties: The menthol and other compounds in peppermint cream act as a natural anesthetic, numbing the breast tissue and reducing pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
Antispasmodic effects: Peppermint's essential oils help relax smooth muscle tissue in the breasts, alleviating cramping and spasms that can occur during lactation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with breastfeeding nipple soreness.
Leaves: Packed with menthol and menthone, which provide analgesic and anti-spasmodic properties to soothe and calm breast pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves and dry them thoroughly to preserve their potency and aroma.
Combine one part dried peppermint leaves with two parts coconut oil in a heat-proof glass bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler or a shallow metal pan set over simmering water for 30 minutes to infuse.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container and discard solids.
Whip the strained oil with one tablespoon of beeswax pellets at room temperature until it reaches a creamy consistency suitable for application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they contain triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and discomfort in the breasts. The cream's active compounds also help to stimulate milk production and promote relaxation, easing tension and pain caused by engorgement or nipple soreness. By soothing and calming the breast tissue, fenugreek creams provide relief from pain and discomfort, allowing new mothers to focus on nurturing their babies without distraction.
remedy_properties: Galactagogue activity: Fenugreek's ability to increase milk supply can help alleviate breast engorgement and discomfort, reducing the need for painful expression or compression.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and irritation in the breasts, providing relief from tenderness, swelling, and redness.

Wound-healing and tissue repair: Fenugreek's antioxidants and bioactive compounds promote wound healing and tissue repair, which can aid in the recovery of breast tissue damage caused by breastfeeding and help soothe nipple cracks or fissures.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fenugreek roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe and reduce inflammation in the breasts, alleviating discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
Seeds: Fenugreek seeds contain a natural hormone called diosgenin, which has been shown to increase milk production and reduce breast pain and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Measure out two tablespoons of dried trigonella foenum graecum powder and add it to one cup of boiling distilled water.
Allow the mixture to steep for twenty minutes before straining off the liquid and setting aside the herb residue.
Combine the cooled herbal infusion with eight ounces of raw shea butter and four ounces of coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Add ten drops of lavender essential oil and blend the mixture thoroughly until it reaches a smooth, creamy consistency.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and allow them to cool before refrigerating for at least six hours or overnight for optimal storage and shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they contain isoflavones, a natural phytoestrogen that mimics the effects of estrogen in the body. Isoflavones help to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with engorgement, nipple soreness, and mastitis. By applying red clover cream topically to the affected area, new mothers can experience relief from breast pain and discomfort, allowing them to focus on bonding with their baby and enjoying the breastfeeding experience without distraction.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover cream contains isoflavones, which have been shown to reduce breast tenderness and discomfort by relaxing the muscles in the breast tissue and reducing inflammation.

Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogens present in red clover cream mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, helping to balance hormone levels and alleviate symptoms associated with breast engorgement and nipple soreness.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Red clover contains various anti-inflammatory compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the breasts by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in isoflavones, roots are used to soothe and calm nipple tenderness and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants and flavonoids, flowers help reduce inflammation and ease breast engorgement and nipple soreness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried red clover flowers, ensuring they are organic and free of contaminants.
Mix the dried flowers with 40 grams of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks to allow for infusion allowing for optimal extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Combine 100 milliliters of the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's ingredients, such as chamazulene and apigenin, work together to reduce swelling and discomfort in the breasts, allowing for a smoother nursing experience. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety associated with breastfeeding, further reducing breast pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and discomfort associated with engorgement and nipple soreness, making it more comfortable for new mothers to breastfeed.
Antispasmodic: Chamomile's ability to relax muscle spasms can help alleviate cramping and tension in the breasts, allowing for easier latch-on and reducing pain during feeding sessions.

Soothing and calming: Chamomile's calming effects can promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and ease stress-related breast tenderness, making it easier for mothers to bond with their babies and enjoy the breastfeeding experience.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high content of apigenin, an antioxidant that helps soothe and calm breast tissue, reducing pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.

Roots: Chamomile roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the breasts, making them a valuable addition to creams for breastfeeding breast pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of matricaria chamomilla and grind them into a fine powder weighing approximately one hundred grams.
Combine the ground chamomile flowers with three tablespoons of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Place the bowl over a pot of simmering water and gently warm it for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until the mixture reaches 160°f to 180°f.
Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping the mixture with an electric mixer for approximately five minutes.
Transfer the whipped chamomile cream to sterilized glass containers and store them in the refrigerator for up to one week or freeze for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Evening primrose creams help with breastfeeding breast pain because they provide a natural, soothing relief to sore and inflamed breasts. The cream's active ingredient, gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), is an omega-6 fatty acid that works to reduce inflammation and promote healing. This allows mothers to feel more comfortable while nursing, reducing the discomfort and distress associated with engorgement, nipple soreness, and mastitis. By providing a calming and nourishing environment for the breast tissue, evening primrose creams help make breastfeeding a more enjoyable and successful experience.
remedy_properties: Gamma-Linolenic Acid (GLA): GLA, an omega-6 fatty acid found in evening primrose oil, helps reduce inflammation and relieve breast pain by regulating prostaglandins, which can contribute to mastitis and discomfort during lactation.

Antioxidants: Evening primrose oil contains antioxidants that help protect the breasts from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy milk supply.

Tocopherol: Tocopherol, a form of vitamin E found in evening primrose oil, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce breast pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding, while also supporting the health of the nipple-areolar complex.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in omega-6 fatty acids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with breast engorgement.
Seeds: Contain gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an essential fatty acid that has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm breast tenderness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh oenothera biennis flowers at dawn on a sunny day and rinse them gently under running water.
Dry the flowers in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or use a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours.
Combine 20g of dried oenothera biennis flowers with 50g of sweet almond oil and infuse for 4 weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids; let the cream cool to room temperature.
Whip the cooled cream with an electric mixer until it forms a smooth consistency, adding 10 drops of lavender essential oil for its calming properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can effectively alleviate discomfort and tension in the breasts. The cream's soothing and calming effects help to reduce swelling, ease congestion, and ease nipple soreness, allowing mothers to enjoy a more comfortable and enjoyable breastfeeding experience. Additionally, lemon balm has natural antibacterial properties that may help to prevent mastitis, a common complication of breastfeeding.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm's antispasmodic property helps to relax the muscles in the breast, reducing breast pain and discomfort associated with latching difficulties or engorgement.

Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the breasts, which can alleviate breast pain and swelling caused by mastitis or nipple trauma during breastfeeding.
Calming and sedative property: Lemon balm's calming and sedative properties help to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which can contribute to breast pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing breast tissue.
Leaves: Containing high levels of rosmarinic acid, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to ease breast pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried melissa officinalis leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered melissa officinalis with 20 grams of beeswax and 30 milliliters of coconut oil in a heat-proof glass measuring cup.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil mixture over low heat on a double boiler for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from heat and add 10 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 5 grams of vitamin e oil, stirring well to combine.
Pour the melted cream into sterilized glass jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they provide a soothing, gentle relief to irritated nipples and breasts. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by nipple soreness or engorgement, allowing mothers to nurse more comfortably and with less distress. Additionally, the cream's emollient texture creates a protective barrier that shields the skin from friction, promoting healthy tissue growth and reducing inflammation further.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow root contains mucilages that help reduce swelling and inflammation, providing relief from sore and painful breasts associated with breastfeeding.

Sotheing and moisturizing properties: The emollient and demulcent properties of marshmallow root create a protective barrier on the skin, soothing and calming irritated tissues, and reducing pain and discomfort.
Antispasmodic properties: Marshmallow root has been traditionally used to relieve cramps and spasms in the breast tissue, helping to ease pain and discomfort caused by breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilages, which help to soothe and calm irritated breast tissue, reducing pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
Leaves: The leaves contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce swelling and ease pain caused by engorgement or mastitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis roots and dry them for at least two weeks in a warm well-ventilated area to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind dried althaea officinalis roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to ensure optimal extraction.
Combine one part powdered althaea officinalis with three parts cold-pressed coconut oil and heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for two hours to infuse the cream.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids, then let it cool before refrigerating or freezing for later use.
Whip the cooled cream with an electric mixer until it thickens to a consistency suitable for application, taking about 10 minutes to achieve optimal texture and emulsification.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The creams contain extracts from the maidenhair tree, which are known to reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms in the breasts. This relief can help alleviate discomfort and pain associated with engorgement, nipple soreness, and other common issues experienced by breastfeeding mothers. As a result, women may find it easier to nurse their babies and enjoy a more comfortable and satisfying breastfeeding experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Maiden hair tree cream has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and discomfort in the breasts, making it an effective natural remedy for breastfeeding-related breast pain.

Antispasmodic properties: The herbal extract in maiden hair tree cream can relax muscles and reduce spasms, providing relief from engorgement, tenderness, and nipple pain often experienced by breastfeeding mothers.

Wound-healing and tissue-regenerating properties: Maiden hair tree has been shown to accelerate wound healing and promote tissue regeneration, which can help soothe and repair damaged skin and nipple tissue caused by breastfeeding-related trauma or poor latching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and reduce breast pain in lactating women.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids, barks have been traditionally used to relieve nipple soreness and breast engorgement.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize clean glass jars and utensils using boiling water for at least 10 minutes.
Combine 100g of coconut oil with 20g of beeswax in a heat-resistant saucepan.
Add 10ml of dried ginkgo biloba extract to the melted mixture, stirring well to combine ingredients.
Stir the mixture continuously over low heat for 5-7 minutes until it reaches 55-60°c temperature.
Pour the warm mixture into prepared jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the breasts. The natural oils in borage, such as gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), help to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort caused by engorgement or nipple soreness. Additionally, the moisturizing qualities of borage creams promote healthy skin and nipple tissue, allowing for a smoother feeding experience and reduced pain during lactation. As a result, using herbal borage creams can provide relief and comfort for breastfeeding mothers.
remedy_properties: Gamma-Linolenic Acid (GLA): Helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with breastfeeding, promoting comfortable milk production and latching.
Omega-6 fatty acids: Nourishes and protects the breast tissue, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by engorgement and nipple soreness.
Vitamins A, C, and E: Antioxidants that help soothe and calm the breasts, promoting healthy skin and tissues, which in turn reduces pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with breastfeeding breast pain.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and antioxidants, these seeds help soothe and calm breast tissue, reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borago officinalis leaves and flowers for a total of 20 grams.
Dry the harvested herbs in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine dried herbs with 100 milliliters of olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 days at room temperature allowing for infusion and extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they provide relief from inflammation and discomfort caused by engorgement. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease the pressure on the breasts, making it easier to nurse comfortably. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects soothe sore nipples and dry skin, promoting a healthy latch and reducing pain associated with breastfeeding. By alleviating discomfort, these creams empower new mothers to continue nursing without interruption or distress.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Reduces inflammation and swelling, easing discomfort and pain associated with engorgement and nipple soreness.
Flavonoids: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the breast tissue, reducing irritation and pain caused by hormonal changes during breastfeeding.

Saponins: Mucilages that create a protective barrier on the skin, shielding it from irritants and allergens while also providing lubrication to reduce friction and discomfort associated with latching issues or nipple thrush.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe breast pain associated with breastfeeding.
Barks: Containing triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to ease breast pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground root powder with 500 milliliters of cooled coconut oil and stir well for 10 minutes until fully incorporated.
Add 100 grams of beeswax to the mixture and heat it in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a temperature of 45 degrees celsius before whipping it with a hand whisk for 5 minutes.
Pour the whipped cream mixture into sterile glass jars and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature of 4 degrees celsius for up to six weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated breast tissue. The cream's gentle heat stimulates blood flow, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with engorgement, cracked nipples, or clogged ducts. Additionally, dandelion's natural analgesic properties provide quick relief from aching breasts, allowing new mothers to focus on bonding with their baby rather than managing pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxacosides, help reduce swelling and discomfort in breasts, making it easier for new mothers to breastfeed.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic effects of dandelion cream can help relax muscle spasms and cramping in the breasts, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.

Pain-relieving properties: Dandelion's analgesic (pain-relieving) compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, can help reduce breast pain, soreness, and engorgement, making it easier for mothers to feed their babies comfortably.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to soothe and calm sore breasts.
Seeds: Dandelion seeds contain a unique combination of fatty acids and sterols that can help to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or using desiccant.
Combine 20g of dried dandelion roots and flowers with 50ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Heat the mixture at low temperature in a double boiler for 2-3 hours to extract herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container and store in refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roselle creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the breasts, reducing discomfort and tenderness. The cream's natural ingredients, such as hibiscus sabdariffa, work to relax the milk ducts and ease engorgement, allowing for a more comfortable and efficient feeding experience. Additionally, roselle creams help to stimulate milk production and improve latch, further alleviating breast pain associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Roselle cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce breast inflammation and swelling, alleviating discomfort and pain caused by engorgement or plugged ducts.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in Roselle cream protect the breasts from damage caused by oxidative stress, promoting healthy milk flow and reducing breast pain associated with nipple soreness or cracked nipples.

Uterotonic properties: Roselle's uterotonic effects help to stimulate contractions that support milk letdown, ease engorgement, and reduce breast tension and pain, making it easier for new mothers to manage breastfeeding challenges.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roselle roots help soothe and reduce inflammation associated with breastfeeding breast pain.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, roselle barks have been traditionally used to alleviate breast engorgement and pain during lactation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hibiscus sabdariffa flowers and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine 2 cups of dried hibiscus flowers with 4 cups of boiling distilled water in a stainless steel pot.
Simmer the mixture for 20-30 minutes then let it cool before straining through cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Add 1 cup of unrefined coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the hibiscus tea mixture while still warm and stir until melted.
Pour the herbal cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and refrigerate for at least 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which alleviate engorgement and discomfort. The curcumin present in turmeric reduces swelling and soothes sore nipples, allowing for more comfortable feeding sessions. Additionally, the cream's warming effect can help increase milk production by stimulating blood flow to the breasts. As a result, nursing mothers may experience relief from the pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding, promoting a smoother and more enjoyable feeding experience for both mother and baby.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, reduce inflammation and swelling in the breasts, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with breastfeeding.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of turmeric help protect the breasts from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing pain and tenderness.
Pain-relieving: Turmeric's analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds work together to reduce breast pain and discomfort, making it easier for breastfeeding mothers to nurse comfortably.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric's rhyzomes contain high amounts of curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with breastfeeding breast pain.

Barks: The barks of the turmeric plant have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including breast pain during lactation, due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots weighing about 100 grams and peel them thoroughly.
Grind the peeled turmeric roots into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of the ground turmeric powder with 50 milliliters of coconut oil in a glass jar and stir until well combined.
Heat the mixture over low heat on a double boiler for 30 minutes to allow for infusion and melting of the ingredients.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken completely before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they contain a natural anti-inflammatory compound that reduces swelling and discomfort. The cream's active ingredients, such as achilleic acid and flavonoids, work to soothe and calm the breasts, providing relief from engorgement, nipple soreness, and mastitis. Additionally, yarrow's antispasmodic properties help to relax the breast tissue, easing tension and pain associated with breastfeeding. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, yarrow creams provide effective natural relief for breastfeeding mothers experiencing breast pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow creams have anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with engorgement, mastitis, and nipple soreness during breastfeeding.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: The antimicrobial agents in yarrow cream help combat bacteria and fungi that can cause breast infections and inflammation, promoting a healthy healing environment for nursing mothers.

Astringent and cicatrisant properties: Yarrow's astringent and cicatrisant properties help reduce breast milk flow, ease engorgement, and promote tissue repair by stimulating the growth of new skin cells, making it an effective remedy for nipple soreness and cracked nipples.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of yarrow are used to make creams because they contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe breast tissue.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers are valued for their ability to ease engorgement and alleviate breast pain due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried achillea millefolium leaves in amounts of 20 grams for every 100 milliliters of cream base you plan to prepare.
Combine the harvested achillea millefolium leaves with 500 milliliters of organic coconut oil in a slow cooker or double boiler and let steep for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids to yield 300 milliliters of infused oil.
Add 100 millilitres of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter to the infused oil and heat in a double boiler until melted and well combined.
Remove the mixture from heat, let cool slightly, then whip with an electric mixer for 10 minutes to achieve a smooth and creamy texture suitable for topical application.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for breastfeeding breast pain are topical creams infused with natural herbs and ingredients specifically designed to soothe and alleviate discomfort associated with breastfeeding. These creams help by reducing inflammation, relieving tension, and promoting relaxation of the breast tissue, ultimately easing pain caused by engorgement, nipple soreness, and clogged ducts. Examples of herbal creams include those containing fenugreek, chamomile, and calendula, which have been shown to effectively reduce breastfeeding-related breast pain, allowing mothers to comfortably continue nursing and enjoy a more positive breastfeeding experience, improving their overall well-being and confidence.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat breastfeeding breast pain with herbal creams is by using a combination of cooling and anti-inflammatory ingredients. Try applying a cream containing peppermint oil, which helps to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort. Alternatively, look for products featuring calendula extract, which promotes wound healing and reduces swelling. Apply the cream to the affected area several times a day as needed to alleviate pain and discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to breastfeeding breast pain that are treated with herbal creams are mastitis, fibrocystic breasts, and cystic acne. These creams often contain ingredients like peppermint, chamomile, and calendula, which help soothe inflammation, reduce swelling, and promote healing. They may also include antimicrobial agents to combat bacterial infections and antifungal properties to prevent yeast overgrowth, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
